Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta VIP Travel Perks Suspended For Members Of Congress Amid Budget Dispute

Extremely long wait times to get through security at US airports has been commonplace in recent weeks as a partial government shutdown has forced airport security workers to work without pay until their portion of the budget can be passed by politicians. As a result, Delta Air Lines said on...

Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals airlines are leveraging their operational challenges during the shutdown to pressure lawmakers on budget negotiations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

14 Hours Nonstop: Inside Virgin Atlantic's New Longest Route

Discover the intriguing backstory behind Virgin's new longest service...

Extended long-haul routes require operational innovations in crew scheduling, aircraft maintenance intervals, and passenger comfort strategies that reshape traditional airline economics.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Five aviation rivalries that define competition in the modern era

Although aviation happens to be one of the most welcoming industries with collaborations, an integral part of what…...

Modern aviation competition increasingly blurs manufacturer-operator-supplier lines, with strategic rivalries driving innovation across aircraft development, network strategy, and technology adoption rather than simple market share battles.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk controls require organizations to formally document and justify acceptance of remaining risks, transforming theoretical safety analysis into operational responsibility.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers

Starcloud has raised $170 million to accelerate development of its next-generation spacecraft, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation as it awaits permission to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network.

Orbital data centers represent a new revenue stream beyond traditional space services, attracting venture capital at scale as latency-sensitive computing moves to LEO infrastructure.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Best Transpacific Airlines (Premium Comfort Revealed)

For years, flying across the Pacific Ocean meant preparing for one of the most exhausting journeys in commercial aviation. A trip between the US West Coast and East Asia could easily stretch beyond ten hours, and passengers often judged the experience by how well they could simply survive the...

Airlines are competing on premium cabin amenities and service quality to justify significantly higher ticket prices on ultra-long-haul transpacific routes where flight duration exceeds passenger endurance thresholds.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Airbus Bird of Prey drone interceptor fires Frankenburg missile on maiden flight

Airbus Defence and Space has completed the first demonstration flight of its Bird of Prey uncrewed interceptor, with…...

Airbus's Bird of Prey demonstrates operational unmanned combat capability, signaling accelerated adoption of autonomous air-to-air systems in modern military aviation arsenals.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation before incidents occur.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ANA Holdings joins Japanese consortium to track CO2 emissions from space

ANA Holdings has joined a Japanese consortium selected to develop satellite and aircraft-based technology for tracking carbon dioxide…...

ANA's satellite-based CO2 monitoring technology could provide airlines with granular emissions data to optimize routes and support carbon accounting compliance.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Finnair to lease Embraer E190-E1 and ATR-72-600 aircraft for Norra operations

A week after announcing its largest order in years, Finnair has signed Letters of Intent (LOI) for the…...

Finnair's leasing strategy for Norra operations suggests capacity additions without major capital expenditure, allowing flexibility to assess regional demand before committing to permanent fleet expansion.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Science finds a place on Artemis 2

While the Artemis 2 mission is primarily a test flight, the four astronauts on board will conduct some science during the nearly 10-day mission.

Artemis 2's dual-purpose design integrates scientific research alongside engineering validation, establishing a template for balancing exploration objectives with data collection on human spaceflight missions.

See SpaceNews →

▶
General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as crucial economic anchors by supporting mixed operations from flight training through commercial service, diversifying revenue beyond passenger traffic.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Do Airlines Still Invest In Premium Economy Instead Of Business Class?

It seems to be one of the use of space aboard aircraft for maximizing revenue.

Premium economy generates higher margins per square foot than business class while capturing price-sensitive premium passengers who won't pay full business fares.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

easyJet Flight Attendant Injured As Turbulence Strikes Shortly After Takeoff

An easyJet cabin crew member required emergency medical assistance.

Severe early-flight turbulence injuries highlight the operational risk window when cabin crew mobility is highest and safety equipment may not yet be fully secured.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Saab CEO: Canada could get its first Gripen fighter in five years

Saab’s chief executive said Canada’s existing aerospace infrastructure sets it apart from previous Gripen customers and could support a faster…...

Canada's established aerospace manufacturing base could compress typical Gripen delivery timelines, creating a competitive advantage over nations building support infrastructure from scratch.

See AeroTime Hub →

▶
Safety Mar 30, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway incursion risks at LAX intensify during high-traffic periods when pilots unfamiliar with exit taxiway layouts may unknowingly cross active runways during departure operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Delta A330 Engine Fire Spews Debris Over São Paulo Airport After Takeoff

The situation caused other flights to be diverted due to the subsequent runway closure.

Engine failures causing debris dispersion and runway closures create cascading operational disruptions affecting multiple flights beyond the incident aircraft itself.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets

While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.

The F-22's sustained air dominance capabilities demonstrate that specialized platform design remains superior to multi-role versatility in specific operational niches.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

One of two drones crashed in Finland identified as Ukrainian

Two drones crashed in southeastern Finland on March 29, 2026, with the Finnish Air Force confirming that at…...

Drone incursions into NATO airspace signal escalating unmanned system threats requiring immediate continental air defense protocol coordination.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flights can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact as traffic demand continues to surge.

See MDPI →

Drones Mar 30, 2026

BRINC Drones – Product Marketing Manager

At BRINC, we are redefining public safety with an innovative ecosystem of life-saving tools. Our...

BRINC is expanding its public safety drone operations beyond traditional aerial platforms, signaling enterprise-level commercialization of specialized UAS for first responders.

Drones Mar 30, 2026

DroneShield Establishes European Headquarters to Accelerate Regional Growth and Sovereign Counter-UAS Capability

DroneShield, a global leader in counter-drone and electronic warfare (EW) solutions, is pleased to announce...

DroneShield's European expansion signals growing regulatory demand for sovereign counter-UAS systems as nations prioritize domestic drone threat detection over reliance on foreign providers.

See sUAS News →

Air Cargo Mar 30, 2026

Why Do Cargo Airlines Still Prefer The Boeing 747 Over Newer Jets?

The new generation of jets has pushed out the icons of old, but one is still hanging around and is actually preferred over many of these new options.

The 747's unmatched cargo volume and deck configuration provide superior economics for freight operators despite higher fuel costs, making payload capacity a greater profit driver than fuel efficiency in cargo aviation.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

"Possible Hijacking": Frontier Passenger Triggers Security Scare After Bomb And Death Threats

A level four alert on flight 2539 in Atlanta.

Security incidents involving bomb threats require airlines to implement costly operational disruptions and investigate passenger behavior patterns to prevent future threats.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Singapore Airlines Smashes Records: Now 23 Daily Australia Flights

The carrier will be the first international airline to serve Sydney's new airport.

Singapore Airlines' expanded Australia network positions it to capture premium leisure and business traffic on the region's highest-yielding routes amid post-pandemic capacity growth.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Indonesia receives final A400M after rerouted delivery flight around Middle East

Indonesia has received its second and final Airbus A400M military transport aircraft, completing a two-aircraft order placed in…...

Indonesia's A400M delivery required Middle East routing adjustments, illustrating how geopolitical tensions increasingly complicate military aircraft logistics and supply chain planning.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

Satellite imaging industry’s next challenge: getting systems to talk to each other

Executives say lack of incentives and interoperability slows ‘tipping and cueing’ across vendors...

Fragmented satellite imaging ecosystems are hindering real-time data sharing between vendors, creating operational inefficiencies that could be solved through standardized interoperability protocols.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 29, 2026

Misawa’s 13th Fighter Squadron Receives First F-35s

Misawa Air Base has received the U.S. Air Force’s first F-35A Lightning II fighters to be permanently based in Japan, beginning the transition from the F-16 to the fifth-gen aircraft. Misawa Air Base, home of the U.S. Air Force’s 35th Fighter Wing, has received its first four F-35A Lightning II...

Japan's F-35A deployment strengthens U.S. Indo-Pacific deterrence capabilities and signals accelerated modernization of allied air forces in a region of growing strategic tension.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi

Aer Lingus has equipped the first aircraft in its fleet with Starlink, delivering ultrafast, reliable Wi-Fi to customers and marking a major step forward for customer experience. Customers travelling from Dublin to New York JFK on flight EI105 today are the first to experience the innovative...

Aer Lingus's Starlink deployment signals a competitive shift toward satellite-based connectivity as a standard amenity, pressuring rivals to modernize IFE infrastructure or risk losing premium-tier passengers.

See Runway Girl Network →

Drones Mar 29, 2026

Unidentified Drones Crash Near Kouvola, Finland, Authorities Investigate Possible Origin

Finnish authorities are investigating reports that one or two unmanned aerial systems havecrashed in southeastern...

Finland's drone incident underscores growing border security vulnerabilities as unmanned systems increasingly breach territorial airspace without clear attribution mechanisms.

See sUAS News →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Op-Ed: Seating architecture must evolve to accommodate power, IFEC

Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Unum VP Business Development, Alan McInnes Moving beyond the traditional considerations of structure, comfort and certification, aircraft seating is a critical integration point for power, in-flight entertainment (IFE) and connectivity systems that...

Modern aircraft seat design must prioritize seamless integration of power and connectivity infrastructure rather than treating these systems as afterthoughts to comfort and structure.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

India inaugurates Noida International Airport, Delhi’s second gateway

India opened the first phase of Noida International Airport (DXN) in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, on March 28, 2026,…...

Noida International Airport expands Delhi's capacity to 120 million annual passengers, positioning India to capture growing Asia-Pacific aviation demand and reduce congestion at existing Indira Gandhi Airport.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged in Iranian strike on Saudi air base

An Iranian missile and drone attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 27, 2026,…...

The vulnerability of large, high-value airborne early warning platforms to coordinated missile-drone attacks signals a critical need for AWACS force structure diversification and enhanced air defense integration.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings

While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.

NASA's focus on accelerating robotic lunar missions reveals a strategic gap in crewed lander development momentum, potentially creating timeline misalignment with Artemis program objectives.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF A-10 strike on Iraqi military base kills seven soldiers

An airstrike attributed to a US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II hit a military clinic at the Habbaniyah…...

Friendly fire incidents involving close air support platforms highlight persistent challenges in combat identification and coordination in congested operational environments.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of advanced air-to-air missile systems by competing contractors suggests accelerated development timelines for next-generation air combat capabilities.

See The Aviationist →
